reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that handling KILLPRIV_V2 stripping in the server is not burdensome, agreed to require FUSE_KILLPRIV_V2 for passthrough inode operations in v3.
reviews.raw_body:
I was thinking from the user perspective, it'd be nicer to not couple
things unless it was absolutely necessary, especially since
passthrough is a per-inode/per-file attribute whereas KILLPRIV_V2 is a
global server configuration (eg server might want passthrough on just
a few files without taking on killpriv responsibility for the whole
filesystem).

But I think everything you wrote makes sense and I don't think
handling the stripping stuff is too burdensome to ask of the server.

For v3, I'll make the change to require FUSE_KILLPRIV_V2 for
FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-05-18.patch_summary: This patch adds a passthrough setattr feature to the FUSE file system, which allows setting attributes directly on the backing inode instead of sending a FUSE_SETATTR request to the server. This is done by checking for passthrough setattr before stripping suid/sgid permissions in the handle_killpriv/handle_killpriv_v2 functions. The patch introduces new functions and macros to support this feature, including fuse_passthrough_setattr and FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_SETATTR.
